body:
```Julia
using Oceananigans
model = HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel(grid=RectilinearGrid(topology=(Bounded, Bounded, Bounded), size=(8, 6, 8), extent=(10, 20, 3)));
model.velocities.u
```

before this PR would give:
```julia
9×6×8 Field{Face, Center, Center} on RectilinearGrid on CPU
├── grid: 8×6×8 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Bounded, Bounded, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── boundary conditions: FieldBoundaryConditions
│   └── west: Open, east: Open, south: ZeroFlux, north: ZeroFlux, bottom: ZeroFlux, top: ZeroFlux, immersed: ZeroFlux
└── data: 15×12×14 OffsetArray(::Array{Float64, 3}, -2:12, -2:9, -2:11) with eltype Float64 with indices -2:12×-2:9×-2:11
    └── max=0.0, min=0.0, mean=0.0
```

after this PR:
```julia
9×6×8 Field{Face, Center, Center} on RectilinearGrid on CPU
├── grid: 8×6×8 RectilinearGrid{Float64, Bounded, Bounded, Bounded} on CPU with 3×3×3 halo
├── boundary conditions: FieldBoundaryConditions
│   └── west: Impenetrable, east: Impenetrable, south: ZeroFlux, north: ZeroFlux, bottom: ZeroFlux, top: ZeroFlux, immersed: ZeroFlux
└── data: 15×12×14 OffsetArray(::Array{Float64, 3}, -2:12, -2:9, -2:11) with eltype Float64 with indices -2:12×-2:9×-2:11
    └── max=0.0, min=0.0, mean=0.0
```
title: Better show for impenetrable boundary condition

body: This PR defines some binary operations involving `ZeroField`s that might be useful, and adds tests for those operations. The issue came up at https://github.com/tomchor/Oceanostics.jl/pull/104
title: Defines some binary operations with `ZeroField`
